summary:
Elderly patients do call 911 when presenting with clinically suspicious signs of an acute stroke, primarily because public health initiatives like FAST and BEFAST have successfully promoted prompt recognition and response among this high-risk group. This behavior directly enables critical interventions—such as timely transportation to designated stroke centers and earlier use of thrombolytic therapies—that improve neurological outcomes if delivered promptly.  
$\boxed{\text{A}}$
